input.idea: Why junior engineers should learn Rust in 2026

research.summary: Rust content on YouTube is saturated with 'Rust vs Go' videos; gap exists for career-angle content aimed at early-career engineers.
trending_angles: Why FAANG teams are migrating to Rust

chosen_angle.title: I Learned Rust in 30 Days — Here's What Nobody Tells Junior Devs
chosen_angle.hook: Rust isn't hard. The way it's taught is broken.
chosen_angle.premise: A pragmatic 30-day roadmap for early-career engineers, framed around career ROI not syntax.
chosen_angle.why_it_wins: Hits the career-angle gap competitors ignore; promises a concrete outcome.

ideation.target_audience: Engineers 0-3 years experience, currently writing JS/Python, considering a systems language

script_writer.hook: Rust isn't hard. The way it's taught is broken — and it's costing junior engineers jobs.
script_writer.cta: If this saved you 3 months of tutorial hell, drop a comment with your day-1 goal.

optimization.title: I Learned Rust in 30 Days — Here's What Nobody Tells Juniors
optimization.description: Rust isn't hard. The way it's taught is broken...

scenes.narration: Rust isn't hard. The way it's taught is broken.
scenes.visual_prompt: Close-up of a frustrated developer at a cluttered desk, multiple monitors showing compiler errors, warm tungsten lighting, shallow depth of field, cinematic 35mm

thumbnail_prompts: Split composition: confused junior dev on left in muted tones, confident version of same dev on right in vibrant orange and teal, bold contrast, cinematic lighting
